summary: Proof ownership for generic AOS contracts separated from the retired embedded product.

entries.contract: The desktop capability map stays backed by current command manifest forms and does not advertise retired status-item or unsupported desktop verbs.
entries.worth: This prevents active desktop guidance from preserving retired experience status-item invocation or claiming unsupported macOS semantics.
replaces: experience status-item menu invoke as active desktop capability
entries.guard: manifest and docs assertions only; no daemon

entries.contract: Agent Terminal provider observation, PTY transport, chrome, and launcher wiring remain neutral toolkit capabilities with no embedded product route.
entries.worth: This preserves the reusable terminal substrate while deleting product launch and renderer ownership.
replaces: embedded Sigil Agent Terminal tests and launcher composition
entries.guard: fake providers and local PTY fixtures only; no AOS daemon

entries.contract: Current producer records map losslessly to neutral canonical agent UI targets and keep projections separate.
entries.worth: This retains cross-surface target normalization without treating a product compact surface as the canonical producer.
entries.guard: fixture-only model test

entries.contract: Interaction grammar and target reacquisition fixtures remain namespace-safe with neutral owner identities.
entries.worth: This keeps ambiguity and stale-ref behavior reusable across consumers.
entries.guard: fixture-only model tests

entries.contract: Context sessions preserve ordered targets, artifacts, keyframes, and safe asset references independently of product capture UI.
entries.worth: This retains the persisted AOS context contract while isolating the one legacy entry-source token at its schema boundary.
replaces: embedded product context recording renderer tests
entries.guard: schema and pure model fixtures only

entries.contract: Work Record adapters, consumers, subjects, verifier projections, repair planning, and workbench state operate on neutral workflow fixtures.
entries.worth: This preserves durable execution evidence without making a product wiki workflow part of AOS authority.
entries.guard: schema and pure model tests; command-dispatch fixtures use temporary files only

entries.contract: UX trees, menu subjects, wiki subjects, entry handles, and workbench shells remain generic descriptor and host contracts.
entries.worth: This retains reusable subject/workbench behavior after removing the embedded product subject adapter.
replaces: embedded product UX-tree and subject renderer tests
entries.guard: schema and pure model tests only

entries.contract: Surface Inspector layout, annotation candidates, semantic controls, marks, segments, and interaction guidance use generic root and canvas identities.
entries.worth: This preserves AOS observability and annotation policy without hard-coding a product root canvas.
replaces: product-root special cases in annotation and Surface Inspector models
entries.guard: pure model and source-contract tests only

entries.contract: Visual object descriptors, mutations, resource lifecycle, form binding, and transform-panel semantics use neutral object and canvas identities.
entries.worth: This preserves reusable visual-object editing after product renderer and stellation suites move out of AOS.
replaces: embedded product avatar and renderer visual-object tests
entries.guard: pure model and DOM fixture tests only

entries.contract: The external scene facade exposes only product-neutral DesktopWorld, bounded Three lifecycle, canvas projection, and visual-object primitives.
entries.worth: This lets external first-party consumers reuse renderer mechanics and editing contracts without copying toolkit source or restoring product authority to AOS.
replaces: embedded product avatar renderer lifecycle tests
entries.guard: dependency-injected model tests only; no daemon, GPU, TCC, or product fixture

entries.contract: The public scene follower validates owner, resource, stage, operation, and bounded NDJSON before forwarding through the daemon envelope.
entries.worth: This proves consumers can hold a connection-scoped DesktopWorld lease without direct socket access or product-specific daemon behavior.
entries.guard: disposable fake Unix socket only; no AOS binary, daemon, GUI, TCC, or GPU

entries.contract: Data-only scene cartridges validate canonical paths, digests, implementation declarations, budgets, local assets, and safe resolution without daemon access.
entries.worth: This proves consumers can package reusable DesktopWorld scenes and interactions without executable cartridge code, remote runtime dependencies, or a competing scene transport.
entries.guard: filesystem fixtures and pure Node validation only; no AOS binary, daemon, GUI, TCC, network, or GPU

entries.contract: Canvas lifecycle, menu activation, semantic targets, telemetry, render metrics, and menu adapters accept neutral consumer identities.
entries.worth: This retains platform surface behavior without product fixture IDs becoming implicit API.
entries.guard: pure model and DOM fixture tests only

entries.contract: Active harness guidance teaches canonical AOS primitives and neutral substrate fixtures while historical reports remain non-authoritative.
entries.worth: This prevents deleted product scenarios from returning through copy-pasteable test guidance.
entries.guard: documentation source assertions only

entries.contract: Experience status emits the status-item-free runtime-context v1 contract from product-neutral v1 manifests, keeps runtime v0 frozen for compatibility, and normalizes legacy manifest v0 without preserving retired activation fields.
entries.worth: This keeps active experience discovery and readback centered on content-root and annotation facts without silently mutating either legacy public contract.
replaces: embedded Sigil experience and status-item behavior tests
entries.guard: fake command responses and temporary state only; no daemon

entries.contract: Canonical daemon request, response, and event schemas strictly type product-neutral status-item actions, dry-run and error responses, and matching event envelopes alongside existing services.
entries.worth: This prevents the public daemon schemas from lagging behind the native host wire surface or weakening unrelated envelope validation.
entries.guard: JSON Schema validation only; no daemon

entries.contract: Canvas cleanup distinguishes live neutral experience ownership from stale locks and removes harness-owned canvases in isolated state.
entries.worth: This preserves cleanup safety without launching or inspecting a product experience.
entries.guard: requires a current native binary but uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT; no shared daemon

entries.contract: Generic subject-family cleanup removes cascading descendants, preserves unrelated canvases, detaches non-cascading children, and is idempotent.
entries.worth: A fake AOS command executes the shared cleanup helper without requiring a daemon or product fixture.
replaces: cleanup helper embedded in the retired real-input product harness
entries.guard: temporary JSON state and fake AOS command only; no daemon, canvas, native input, or TCC

entries.contract: The shared cleanup helper integrates with real canvas cascade and input-region retirement in isolated AOS state.
entries.worth: This is the bounded native integration counterpart to the deterministic helper model.
entries.guard: requires a current native binary and isolated daemon; excluded from static publication validation

entries.contract: Visible-surface audit joins neutral logical surfaces, native windows, ownership, and duplicate keys without product inference.
entries.worth: This proves display diagnostics remain generic after product IDs are removed.
entries.guard: requires a current native binary and isolated daemon; synthetic external-window fixture only

entries.contract: Surface Inspector requests and validates see bundles through a neutral harness canvas and isolated daemon.
entries.worth: This keeps the cross-layer bundle proof without a product root canvas.
entries.guard: requires a current native binary and Screen Recording; isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT only

entries.contract: The manual display battery launches only generic Surface Inspector and Spatial Telemetry components.
entries.worth: This prevents a display diagnostic launcher from teaching product presence as AOS acceptance.
entries.guard: manual live visual check against the shared daemon; never run in default validation

entries.contract: A toolkit wiki workbench suspends and resumes through the warm utility-surface lifecycle without duplicate native canvas creation.
entries.worth: This retains the generic lifecycle observation formerly hidden in a product-named smoke.
entries.guard: requires explicit shared-runtime approval and a current native binary; mutates harness-owned canvases

entries.contract: Wiki mutation parser and external dispatch behavior use neutral subject names and reject extra arguments.
entries.worth: This keeps command coverage independent of a product wiki namespace.
entries.guard: uses temporary wiki fixtures and command fakes

entries.contract: Conditional wiki writes are bounded, atomic, owner-only, symlink-safe, hash-guarded, schema-valid, and content-redacted.
entries.worth: This lets first-party consumers publish reviewed knowledge without private socket access or silent lost updates.
entries.guard: uses disposable wiki state, a fake reindex executable for model tests, and the current public CLI for daemonless dispatch; no daemon, TCC, native input, or shared runtime mutation
